mnemonics.title: Remember Key Environment Variables
mnemonics.content:
PATH = P aws X (Search path for commands)
HOME = H OME directory
SHELL = SH ELL interpreter
PWD = P rint W orking D irectory
USER = U ser NAME

acronyms.expansion: Personal Access Through Executables in X
acronyms.usage: Directories where system searches for commands to execute
Current directory location in the filesystem
Command to display or modify environment
Command to convert shell variable to environment variable

visualTips.visualization: Think of environment variables as DNA passed from parent cell (parent process) to child cell (child process). Parent's DNA (environment) is copied to child.
Imagine PATH as a treasure map with X marks the spot. When you type 'ls', system checks each directory on the list to find 'ls' executable.
Shell variables are like money in your wallet (only you can use). Environment variables are like your credit card (shared with authorized users/processes).

analogies.analogy: Like a person's name tag, preferences, and settings that follow them everywhere they go.
Like a school's classroom list - teacher checks each room in order until finding the right class.
Like publishing a document - making it available to others (child processes) instead of keeping it as a private draft.
